injuryUpdates.injuryType: Split cheekbone / facial injury from training collision
injuryUpdates.expectedReturn: Expected to be fit for the tournament opener vs Egypt on 2026-06-16

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Last Napoli appearance before training-ground collision; ruled out of May 12 Bologna fixture

injuryUpdates.source: Fox Sports, Goal.com, beIN Sports - May 12-15, 2026
injuryUpdates.notes: Suffered a split cheekbone in a head-to-head collision at Napoli training; required four stitches. Antonio Conte confirmed no head trauma. Named in Belgium's 26-man squad on May 15 despite the setback. Captain and creative fulcrum — health is the single biggest variable for Belgium's tournament ceiling.

injuryUpdates.injuryType: Hip / hamstring / ankle complaints — chronic fitness issues
injuryUpdates.expectedReturn: Garcia says five weeks to reach full fitness — targeting June 16 opener

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Scored only goal of 2026 season as substitute; just 69 minutes of competitive football in 2026

injuryUpdates.source: ESPN, Al Jazeera, Washington Post, France24, Football-Italia - May 14-15, 2026
injuryUpdates.notes: Situation has deteriorated since April. Played only ~69 competitive minutes all of 2026 (seven Napoli substitute appearances). Hip problem on top of earlier hamstring; spent five weeks rehabbing in Belgium and reportedly tweaked ankle in training at Bosuil (Antwerp). Garcia conceded he 'has recovered but is out of shape' — coach is openly gambling on fitness. Still named in squad as all-time top scorer but starting role for Egypt opener is in serious doubt.

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Returned to Arsenal lineup ahead of season's end; included in Belgium's final 26-man squad

injuryUpdates.source: beIN Sports, FotMob, ESPN squad announcement - May 15, 2026
injuryUpdates.notes: Recovered from the March hip injury and named in Belgium's 26-man squad on May 15. Provides crucial frontline depth given Lukaku's fitness questions.

squadChanges.details: Dropped from Belgium's final 26-man squad after a poor club season (1 goal in 24 Serie A games at Juventus, spent extended stretches on the bench).
squadChanges.source: Athlon Sports, FotMob, beIN Sports - May 15, 2026

squadChanges.details: Dropped from Belgium's final 26-man squad due to ongoing injury struggles at Chelsea.
Surprise omission despite a standout Eredivisie season at Ajax (17 goals, 12 assists in 31 matches).
17-year-old midfielder who debuted in the March 5-2 win over the United States was not retained for the final 26.

squadChanges.source: ESPN, beIN Sports squad announcement - May 15, 2026

squadChanges.details: Lille forward included in the final 26-man squad as part of the next-generation attacking pool.
Strasbourg forward included in the final 26-man squad.

bettingOddsUpdate.notes: Belgium priced at roughly 4/11 to win Group G outright; -250 at BetMGM. Heavy favorites to advance from Group G alongside Egypt, Iran and New Zealand, but mid-tier in the outright market behind Spain, France, England, Brazil and Argentina.

coachUpdate.notes: Garcia named the final 26-man squad on May 15 and remains firmly in charge.

summary: Belgium announced the final 26-man squad on May 15, 2026, and the headline story is fitness: De Bruyne picked up a split cheekbone from a training collision in early May (no head trauma but missed Napoli's last league outing), while Lukaku has played only ~69 competitive minutes all year and Garcia openly admitted he is 'gambling' on the striker's fitness for the tournament opener. Trossard is back and included; Openda (poor Juventus form), Lavia (injury), and surprise omission Mika Godts (despite huge Ajax season) headline a wave of cuts that marks the symbolic end of the Golden Generation. Belgium is the strong group favorite (~4/11) but no warm-up matches have been played since the April 16 data cutoff — the Croatia friendly in Rijeka on June 2 is the next test.
